words.def: to use power or influence to affect somebody/something
words.example: He exerted all his authority to make them accept the plan.

words.def: the state of being sent to live in another country that is not your own, especially for political reasons or as a punishment
words.example: a place of exile

words.def: a way out of a public building or vehicle
words.example: Where's the exit?

words.def: the act of spending or using money; an amount of money spent
words.example: a reduction in public/government/military expenditure

words.def: based on new ideas, forms or methods that are used to find out what effect they have
words.example: The school's experimental teaching methods include letting the children decide what to study.

words.def: to be no longer legally acceptable because the period of time for which it could be used has ended
words.example: When does your driving licence expire?

words.def: clear and easy to understand, so that you have no doubt what is meant
words.example: He gave me very explicit directions on how to get there.

words.def: clearly or directly, so that the meaning is easy to understand
words.example: The report states explicitly that the system was to blame.

words.def: a situation in which somebody treats somebody else in an unfair way, especially in order to make money from their work
words.example: the exploitation of children

words.def: easily able or likely to explode
words.example: an explosive device (= a bomb)

words.def: a short passage from a book, piece of music, etc. that gives you an idea of what the whole thing is like
words.example: The following extract is taken from her new novel.

words.def: a person whose opinions, especially about religion or politics, are extreme, and who may do things that are violent or illegal for what they believe
words.example: left-wing/right-wing/political/religious extremists

words.def: to make an action or a process possible or easier
words.example: The new trade agreement should facilitate more rapid economic growth.

words.def: a small group of people within a larger one, whose members have some different aims and beliefs to those of the larger group
words.example: There are rival factions within the administration.

words.def: a department or group of related departments in a college or university
words.example: the Faculty of Law

words.def: to become or to make something become paler or less bright
words.example: The curtains had faded in the sun.

words.def: the quality of treating people equally or in a way that is reasonable
words.example: The fairness of the judicial system is being questioned.

words.def: causing or ending in death
words.example: a fatal accident/blow/illness

words.def: the things, especially bad things, that will happen or have happened to somebody/something
words.example: The fate of the three men is unknown.

words.def: making people have a good opinion of somebody/something
words.example: She made a favourable impression on his parents.

words.def: an action or a piece of work that needs skill, strength or courage
words.example: The tunnel is a remarkable feat of engineering.

words.def: having or based on the belief that women should have the same rights and opportunities as men
words.example: feminist demands/ideas/theories

words.def: the part of food that helps to keep a person healthy by keeping the bowels working and moving other food quickly through the body
words.example: dietary fibre

words.def: angry and aggressive in a way that is frightening
words.example: a fierce dog

words.def: a device containing paper, sand, chemicals, etc. that a liquid or gas is passed through in order to remove any materials that are not wanted
words.example: an air/oil filter

words.def: in good health
words.example: ‘How are you?’ ‘Fine, thanks.’

words.def: a gun that can be carried
words.example: The police were issued with firearms.

words.def: healthy and strong, especially because you do regular physical exercise
words.example: Top athletes have to be very fit.

words.def: a sports event that has been arranged to take place on a particular date and at a particular place
words.example: There are plans to make the race an annual fixture.

words.def: a mistake in something that means that it is not correct or does not work correctly
words.example: The argument is full of fundamental flaws.

words.def: having a flaw; not perfect or correct
words.example: seriously/fundamentally/fatally flawed

words.def: to leave a person or place very quickly, especially because you are afraid of possible danger
words.example: She burst into tears and fled.

words.def: a group of military ships commanded by the same person
words.example: a fleet of destroyers

words.def: the soft substance between the skin and bones of animal or human bodies
words.example: The trap had cut deeply into the rabbit's flesh.

words.def: the ability to change to suit new conditions or situations
words.example: The new system offers a much greater degree of flexibility in the way work is organized.

words.def: to develop quickly and become successful or common
words.example: Few businesses are flourishing in the present economic climate.

words.def: a liquid; a substance that can flow
words.example: body fluids (= for example, blood)

words.def: part of a film showing a particular event
words.example: old film footage of the moon landing

words.def: a person who comes from a different country
words.example: The fact that I was a foreigner was a big disadvantage.

words.def: to put a lot of effort into making something successful or strong so that it will last
words.example: a move to forge new links between management and workers

words.def: a series of letters, numbers or symbols that represent a rule or law
words.example: This formula is used to calculate the area of a circle.

words.def: to create or prepare something carefully, giving particular attention to the details
words.example: formulate something, to formulate a policy/theory/plan/proposal

words.def: away from a place; out
words.example: They set forth at dawn.

words.def: going to happen, be published, etc. very soon
words.example: the forthcoming elections

words.def: to encourage something to develop
words.example: The club's aim is to foster better relations within the community.

words.def: easily broken or damaged
words.example: fragile china/glass/bones

words.def: formal permission given by a company to somebody who wants to sell its goods or services in a particular area; formal permission given by a government to somebody who wants to operate a public service as a business
words.example: a franchise agreement/company

words.def: in an honest and direct way that people might not like
words.example: He spoke frankly about the ordeal.

words.def: feeling annoyed and impatient because you cannot do or achieve what you want
words.example: It's very easy to get frustrated in this job.

words.def: causing you to feel annoyed and impatient because you cannot do or achieve what you want
words.example: It's frustrating to have to wait so long.

words.def: the feeling of being frustrated
words.example: in frustration, Dave thumped the table in frustration.

words.def: practical and useful; with little or no decoration
words.example: Bathrooms don't have to be purely functional.

words.def: the activity of collecting money for a charity or organization, often by organizing social events or entertainments
words.example: The hospice is planning a major fundraising event for June.

words.def: a ceremony, often a religious one, for burying or cremating (= burning) a dead person
words.example: Hundreds of people attended the funeral.

words.def: a unit for measuring liquid. In the UK, Canada and other countries it is equal to about 4.5 litres; in the US it is equal to about 3.8 litres. There are four quarts in a gallon.
words.example: The tankers carried 130 000 gallons of fuel.

words.def: the activity of playing games of chance for money and of betting on horses, etc.
words.example: online/internet gambling

words.def: a meeting of people for a particular purpose
words.example: a social/family gathering

words.def: a long, steady look at somebody/something
words.example: He met her gaze (= looked at her while she looked at him).

words.def: equipment in a vehicle that changes the relation between engine speed (or pedal speed on a bicycle) and the speed of the wheels moving forwards or backwards
words.example: Careless use of the clutch may damage the gears.

words.def: shared by, including or typical of a whole group of things; not specific
words.example: ‘Vine fruit’ is the generic term for currants and raisins.

words.def: the murder of a large number of people from a particular nation or ethnic group, with the aim of destroying that nation or group
words.example: Refugees gave accounts of the mass genocide.

words.def: a quick look
words.example: to take/have a glance at the newspaper headlines

words.def: a sight of somebody/something for a very short time, when you do not see the person or thing completely
words.example: glimpse (of somebody/something), He caught a glimpse of her in the crowd.

words.def: deserving or bringing great success and making somebody/something famous
words.example: We congratulate you on this glorious victory.

words.def: great success that brings somebody praise and honour and makes them famous
words.example: Olympic glory in the 100 metres

words.def: the activity of governing a country or controlling a company or an organization; the way in which a country is governed or a company or institution is controlled
words.example: He emphasized the company's commitment to high standards of corporate governance.

words.def: an attractive quality of movement that is smooth and done with control; a simple and beautiful quality
words.example: She moves with the natural grace of a ballerina.

words.def: a strong hold of somebody/something or control over somebody/something
words.example: I grabbed him, but he slipped from my grasp.

words.def: very serious and important; giving you a reason to feel worried
words.example: The police have expressed grave concern about the missing child's safety.

words.def: the force that attracts objects in space towards each other, and that on the earth pulls them towards the centre of the planet, so that things fall to the ground when they are dropped
words.example: Newton’s law of gravity

words.def: a pattern of straight lines, usually crossing each other to form squares
words.example: New York’s grid of streets

words.def: a very sad feeling, especially when somebody dies
words.example: She was overcome with grief when her husband died.

words.def: a wide smile
words.example: She gave a broad grin.

words.def: to break or press something into very small pieces between two hard surfaces or using a special machine
words.example: to grind coffee/corn

words.def: an act of holding somebody/something tightly; a particular way of doing this
words.example: Keep a tight grip on the rope.

words.def: being the total amount of something before anything is taken away
words.example: gross weight (= including the container or wrapping)

words.def: a member of a small group of soldiers who are not part of an official army and who fight against official soldiers, usually to try to change the government
words.example: urban guerrillas (= those who fight in towns)

words.def: help or advice that is given to somebody, especially by somebody older or with more experience
words.example: guidance for teachers on how to use video in the classroom

words.def: the unhappy feelings caused by knowing or thinking that you have done something wrong
words.example: She had feelings of guilt about leaving her children and going to work.

words.def: the tube in the body through which food passes when it leaves the stomach
words.example: It can take up to 72 hours for food to pass through the gut.

words.def: to describe somebody/something as being very good or special, especially in newspapers, etc.
words.example: be hailed (as) something, The conference was hailed as a great success.

words.def: at an equal distance between two points; in the middle of a period of time
words.example: It's about halfway between London and Bristol.

words.def: an act of stopping the movement or progress of somebody/something
words.example: Work came to a halt when the machine broke down.

words.def: the amount of something that can be held in one hand
words.example: a handful of rice

words.def: the way that somebody deals with or treats a situation, a person, an animal, etc.
words.example: I was impressed by his handling of the affair.

words.def: easy to use or to do
words.example: a handy little tool

words.def: the act of annoying or worrying somebody by putting pressure on them or saying or doing unpleasant things to them
words.example: sexual/racial harassment

words.def: the machines and electronic parts in a computer or other electronic system
words.example: We supply computer hardware to businesses.

words.def: a state of peaceful existence and agreement
words.example: social/racial harmony

words.def: cruel, severe and unkind
words.example: The punishment was harsh and unfair.

words.def: the time of year when the crops are gathered in on a farm, etc.; the act of cutting and gathering crops
words.example: harvest time

words.def: a very strong feeling of dislike for somebody/something
words.example: He looked at me with intense hatred.

words.def: if the ghost of a dead person haunts a place, people say that they have seen it there
words.example: A headless rider haunts the country lanes.

words.def: something that can be dangerous or cause damage
words.example: a fire/safety hazard

words.def: if a feeling or an effect heightens, or something heightens it, it becomes stronger or increases
words.example: Tension has heightened after the recent bomb attack.

words.def: the history, traditions, buildings and objects that a country or society has had for many years and that are considered an important part of its character
words.example: Spain’s rich cultural heritage

words.def: a system, especially in a society or an organization, in which people are organized into different levels of importance from highest to lowest
words.example: the social/political hierarchy

words.def: receiving or involving a lot of attention and discussion in the media
words.example: a high-profile campaign

words.def: something that you say or do in an indirect way in order to show somebody what you are thinking
words.example: He gave a broad hint (= one that was obvious) that he was thinking of retiring.

words.def: the country where a person was born
words.example: Many refugees have been forced to flee their homeland.

words.def: a curved piece of metal, plastic or wire for hanging things on, catching fish with, etc.
words.example: a picture/curtain/coat hook

words.def: believing that something you want will happen
words.example: hopeful (that…), I feel hopeful that we'll find a suitable house very soon.

words.def: the furthest that you can see, where the sky seems to meet the land or the sea
words.example: The sun sank below the horizon.

words.def: a hard pointed part that grows, usually in pairs, on the heads of some animals, such as sheep and cows. Horns are often curved.
words.example: a large bull with curved horns

words.def: a person who is captured and held prisoner by a person or group, and who may be injured or killed if people do not do what the person or group is asking
words.example: Three children were taken hostage during the bank robbery.

words.def: aggressive or unfriendly and ready to argue or fight
words.example: The speaker got a hostile reception from the audience.

words.def: aggressive or unfriendly feelings or behaviour
words.example: There was a barely veiled hostility in her tone.

words.def: connected with helping people who are suffering and improving the conditions that they are living in
words.example: to provide humanitarian aid to the war zone

words.def: people in general
words.example: He was found guilty of crimes against humanity.

words.def: showing you do not think that you are as important as other people
words.example: Be humble enough to learn from your mistakes.

words.def: the process of showing, proving or recognizing who or what somebody/something is
words.example: The identification of the crash victims was a long and difficult task.

words.def: based on or connected with an ideology
words.example: ideological differences

words.def: a set of ideas that an economic or political system is based on
words.example: Marxist/capitalist ideology

words.def: a rude way to refer to somebody who you think is very stupid
words.example: When I lost my passport, I felt such an idiot.

words.def: a lack of knowledge or information about something
words.example: They fought a long battle against prejudice and ignorance.

words.def: language that produces pictures in the minds of people reading or listening
words.example: poetic imagery

words.def: extremely large or great
words.example: There is still an immense amount of work to be done.

words.def: likely to happen very soon
words.example: the imminent threat of invasion
